panels.description: Per-interface network traffic (receive and transmit) in bits per second
panels.title: Network Traffic Basic

panels.description: CPU time spent busy vs idle, split by activity type
panels.title: CPU Basic

panels.description: Percentage of filesystem space used for each mounted device
panels.title: Disk Space Used Basic
Tunneled vs Direct Traffic (proof)
panels.description: Requests arriving with X-SSH-Tunnel-Origin (set by the client ONLY when traffic actually traverses the established SSH tunnel). Non-zero 'via SSH tunnel' is proof the tunnel is up and carrying traffic.

panels.title: % Traffic via Tunnel
panels.description: Share of backend requests that arrived through the SSH tunnel.

panels.title: Header Anomalies (tunnel w/o established-at)
panels.description: Requests claiming tunnel origin but missing X-Tunnel-Established-At. Should be ~0; non-zero implies client/header inconsistency or spoofing.

panels.title: SSH Connection / Auth Attempts (proxy key lookups)
panels.description: Each new SSH connection to the proxy triggers one AuthorizedKeysCommand -> argus-cli -> GET /ssh/keys. This rate approximates SSH connection/auth-attempt rate on the proxy host.

panels.title: Tunnel Registrations & Config Fetches
panels.description: New client SSH key registrations and proxy-config fetches. Spikes indicate clients (re)establishing tunnels.

panels.title: Tunneled Requests by Endpoint
panels.description: Top backend endpoints receiving traffic through the SSH tunnel.

panels.title: Tunnel Client Requests by User-Agent
panels.description: Requests from the tunnel-aware client UA vs the plain client UA.

Tunneled requests attributed to the Jenkins build that originated them (X-Argus-Build-Id, formatted job/path#buildNumber). Click a series/point and choose 'Open Jenkins build' to jump to the run. The 'unknown' bucket (non-tunnel or pre-attribution clients) is excluded.
panels.title: Tunneled Traffic by Build / Job
Proxy Bandwidth (NIC)
panels.description: Per-interface bandwidth on the proxy host. REQUIRES node_exporter running on the proxy, scraped under job=$proxy_job.

panels.title: Proxy CPU Busy
panels.description: Proxy host CPU utilisation. Requires node_exporter on the proxy.

panels.title: Proxy Memory Used
panels.description: Proxy host memory utilisation. Requires node_exporter on the proxy.

panels.title: Proxy Active TCP Connections
panels.description: Established TCP connections on the proxy (node_exporter netstat) — a rough proxy for concurrent active SSH tunnel sessions.

panels.title: Proxy Disk Space Used
panels.description: Filesystem usage on the proxy host. Requires node_exporter on the proxy.
